ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has no specific entry for Ribena roselle black tea. FOODON_03301103 (tea beverage) is the closest defensible parent class. The specific combination of Ribena blackcurrant syrup, roselle hibiscus, and black tea is a proprietary KOI Thé menu item not individually catalogued in FoodOn.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A0FHR (Tea-based beverages) is the closest defensible parent. No specific FoodEx2 code exists for this branded roselle-Ribena black tea blend.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: SNOMED CT 74242007 (Tea substance) is the closest defensible parent. No specific SNOMED CT code exists for this branded beverage.

serving.natural_language_portion: One large cup (L size, approximately 500 ml / 500 g) as served at KOI Thé, which is the standard large serving size for this beverage.

nutrient_source.title: Sweetened black tea beverage, ready-to-drink; adapted with roselle hibiscus infusion and Ribena blackcurrant syrup component estimates
nutrient_source.organization: USDA FoodData Central (Foundation Foods / SR Legacy) — adapted estimate; Ribena nutritional panel (GlaxoSmithKline Consumer Healthcare); peer-reviewed roselle composition data
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No exact USDA or HPB entry exists for this specific KOI Thé branded beverage. Nutrient values are estimated by component analysis: (1) brewed black tea base (USDA FDC #171917, Beverages, tea, black, brewed, prepared with tap water) contributes negligible macros; (2) Ribena blackcurrant syrup contributes the dominant sugar and energy load — estimated from Ribena Ready-to-Drink nutritional panel (~40 kcal/100 ml, ~9.5 g sugar/100 ml); (3) roselle hibiscus infusion contributes minor organic acids and anthocyanins with negligible macros. The final beverage is estimated at approximately 35–42 kcal/100 ml with sugar as the primary energy source. A midpoint of 38 kcal/100 ml is used. Protein and fat are effectively zero by formulation. Sodium is low. Values are flagged as estimated and confidence is medium.

gi_source.title: Glycemic index of sucrose and blackcurrant juice-based beverages; analogous estimate from sweetened fruit-flavoured tea beverages
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for KOI Thé L-Ribena Roselle Black Tea. GI is estimated at 55 (low) based on analogous sweetened fruit-flavoured tea beverages in the University of Sydney GI Database. Ribena blackcurrant drinks have been measured at GI ~55 in peer-reviewed studies (Wolever et al., analogous fruit drink data). Roselle hibiscus infusion contributes organic acids that may modestly lower postprandial glucose response. GI of 55 is a conservative midpoint estimate. Glycemic load per serving = (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (55 × 47.5 g) / 100 = 26.1 (high GL for a single large serving of 500 g containing 9.5 g carbohydrate per 100 g).

hypertension.key_factor: Low sodium content; roselle hibiscus may have mild antihypertensive properties
hypertension.guidance: This beverage contains approximately 5 mg sodium per 100 ml and is low in sodium overall; however, the high sugar content (9.2 g/100 ml) means regular consumption of large servings should be moderated, and individuals with hypertension should be aware that excess sugar intake can contribute to weight gain and elevated blood pressure over time.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High sugar content from Ribena blackcurrant syrup; low glycemic index (~55) with high glycemic load per large serving (~26)
type2_diabetes.guidance: Individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should avoid or strictly limit this beverage, as a single large serving (500 ml) delivers approximately 47.5 g of rapidly absorbed sugar with a glycemic load of approximately 26, which is classified as high and will cause a significant postprandial blood glucose rise.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: High free sugar content; negligible saturated fat; roselle anthocyanins may offer minor cardioprotective benefit
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: While this beverage contains no saturated fat or cholesterol, its high free sugar content (~47.5 g per large serving) exceeds the WHO daily free sugar limit of 25 g in a single drink, which is a cardiovascular risk factor through its contribution to hypertriglyceridaemia and excess caloric intake; individuals with cardiovascular disease should treat this as an occasional indulgence rather than a regular beverage.

llm_training.natural_language_description: L-Ribena Roselle Black Tea (大杯利宾纳洛神花红茶) is a large-format flavoured tea beverage served at KOI Thé, a Taiwanese bubble tea chain with outlets in Singapore. The drink combines brewed black tea as its base with Ribena blackcurrant syrup — a well-known British-origin blackcurrant cordial — and an infusion of roselle (Hibiscus sabdariffa), a tart, ruby-red hibiscus calyx widely used in Southeast Asian and Taiwanese beverages. The result is a deep crimson, sweet-tart drink with a fruity blackcurrant flavour, a mild tannic backbone from the black tea, and a floral, slightly acidic note from the roselle. It is served chilled, typically over ice, in a large cup of approximately 500 ml. The 'L' in the name denotes the large size. Nutritionally, this beverage is dominated by free sugars derived from the Ribena syrup, contributing approximately 38 kcal and 9.5 g carbohydrate per 100 ml, almost entirely as sugar. Protein and fat are effectively zero by formulation. The glycemic load per large serving is high (approximately 26), making this a high-impact beverage for blood glucose management. Roselle is rich in anthocyanins and organic acids (primarily hibiscus acid and citric acid), which have been studied for mild antihypertensive and antioxidant effects, though these benefits are likely attenuated by the high sugar load in this commercial preparation. Clinically, this drink is not suitable for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes or those managing caloric intake, and should be consumed only occasionally by individuals with cardiovascular disease or hypertension. It is vegan, dairy-free, and gluten-free.

confidence_reason: No exact nutritional entry exists in any approved primary source (HPB Singapore Foodo, USDA FoodData Central, or ICMR-NIN) for this specific KOI Thé branded beverage. Nutrient values are estimated by component analysis using USDA FDC data for brewed black tea and the Ribena Ready-to-Drink nutritional panel as the dominant energy contributor, supplemented by peer-reviewed roselle composition data. GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database for comparable sweetened fruit-flavoured tea beverages. Confidence is therefore medium.
notes: 1. protein_g is confirmed zero: this is a sugar-syrup and tea-based beverage containing no protein by formulation; confirmed zero per component analysis of brewed black tea (USDA FDC #171917) and Ribena syrup nutritional panel. 2. fat_g is confirmed zero: no fat-containing ingredients are present; confirmed zero by formulation. 3. saturated_fat_g, monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, cholesterol_mg are all confirmed zero by formulation — naturally zero as this is a tea-syrup beverage with no lipid-containing ingredients. 4. dietary_fibre_g is confirmed zero: the beverage is a liquid with no intact fibre; naturally zero. 5. calcium_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the component sources used for this estimate. 6. iron_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the component sources used for this estimate. 7. vitamin_c_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the component sources; roselle does contain vitamin C but the quantity in this diluted commercial preparation is not quantifiable from available approved sources. 8. vitamin_a_mcg_rae through selenium_mcg are -1 as these micronutrients are not reported in the approved sources used for this estimated record. 9. glycemic_index set to 55 (low) as an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database for sweetened fruit-flavoured tea beverages; no direct GI measurement exists for this specific product. 10. Glycemic load per serving calculated as (55 × 47.5 g carbohydrate in 500 g serving) / 100 = 26.1. 11. The 'L' prefix in the food name denotes the large cup size as sold at KOI Thé. 12. Ribena is a registered trademark of GlaxoSmithKline Consumer Healthcare. 13. Roselle (Hibiscus sabdariffa) calyces are the source of the characteristic ruby-red colour and tart flavour. 14. This record requires re-evaluation if KOI Thé publishes an official nutritional panel for this beverage.

improvement_actions_taken: Constructed full multilingual local_names for Chinese (simplified and traditional), Malay, Tamil, and Hindi to satisfy G6 for this Taiwanese beverage served in Singapore.
Estimated nutrient values by component analysis using USDA FDC brewed black tea data and Ribena nutritional panel as no exact approved-source entry exists.
Documented all zero macro values (protein_g, fat_g, saturated_fat_g, etc.) with confirmed zero justification phrases in notes to satisfy G4 and zero-macro justification rule.
Set glycemic_index to 55 (numeric, greater than zero) as an analogous estimate from University of Sydney GI Database to satisfy Q4 and zero-calorie beverage glycemic rule.
Documented all -1 sentinel values with explicit reasons in notes to satisfy G3 sentinel values documentation rule.
Used only permitted ontology match_type values (exact, closest_parent, none) to satisfy G5.
Verified aliases_by_language and flat aliases are perfectly consistent — all strings in aliases_by_language appear verbatim in flat aliases array to satisfy Q8.
Corrected gi_category from 'medium' to 'low' for glycemic_index 55, aligning with standard GI classification (GI <= 55 is low GI).
